hook.text: Brazil built the world's biggest stadium for their World Cup final. Then suffered their worst nightmare.

buildup.text: The 1950 final round needed only a draw for Brazil against tiny Uruguay. 200,000 fans expected a party.

discovery.text: Uruguay scored twice in the second half to win 2-1. The Maracana fell silent.

twist.text: Some fans reportedly died of shock in the stadium. The defeat traumatized an entire nation for decades.

climax.text: Brazil changed their jersey color from white to yellow after the loss—white reminded them of defeat.

punchline.text: 200,000 fans went silent. Uruguay won the impossible final.

quiz.question: What happened in the 1950 World Cup final?
options: Uruguay beat Brazil 2-1 in front of 200,000 fans
Brazil won their first World Cup
The match ended in a draw
